page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

The underlying Intel CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. These hardware features translate to strong benchmark performance in specialized tasks. In testing, the instance achieved top-tier results in Passmark CPU extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. Multi-core performance is highly competitive, as demonstrated by strong scores in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing.

Memory subsystem benchmarks show strong read and write bandwidth, reaching 4103.87 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 277.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. This high memory throughput supports demanding database operations and high-throughput web serving, with Redis SET operations reaching 70.46 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 86.82 GB/sec. Additionally, the AMX instructions enable efficient CPU-based AI workloads, showing strong performance in prompt processing for small and medium LLM models.

Given its balanced resource allocation and high memory bandwidth, the n4-standard-64 offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. It is highly suited for software compilation pipelines, database hosting, static web serving at scale, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated Intel vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 256 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but features a large 260 MB L3 cache and advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core performance, with high memory bandwidth and excellent throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. The instance also excels in CPU-based LLM prompt processing and software compilation. It is highly suited for database operations, CI/CD pipelines, web serving, and medium-scale CPU inference workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a general-purpose server with 64 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and advanced AMX/AVX-512 instructions, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-64 is a 64-vCPU, 256 GB RAM general-purpose server featuring Intel AMX and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM (4 GB per vCPU)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput Redis database performance
Excellent multi-core capabilities for software compilation and ray tracing
Well-suited for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
